_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Using computers and computer systems (including hardware and software) to program, write software, set up functions, enter data, or process information.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in writing Python code for data processing, analysis, and machine learning workflows, including data manipulation with pandas, numerical computation with NumPy, and implementing ML pipelines.
comments: WIDAI TF domain is entirely within this O*NET activity. TF-S-001 (Python), TF-S-002 (SQL), TF-S-009 (containers), plus all analytical and engineering tasks that use computers. WIDAI defines domain-specific computer work in extensive depth.
_comment_atlas_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (nist band: roughly as similar as dissimilar). The moderate STS score is consistent with a Superset relationship where the O*NET element covers significantly broader ground than the WIDAI KSA. The shared conceptual territory is real but represents a fraction of the O*NET element's scope, compressing the similarity signal.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8488 indicates lower token-level semantic alignment. Precision and Recall are balanced, indicating roughly symmetric semantic coverage between the two descriptions. Balanced P/R for a Superset is plausible when the O*NET element's broader scope uses vocabulary that diverges from WIDAI's domain-specific terms, compressing both measures toward similar values.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (99.38%) — the model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other. Neutral is common for Superset pairs where the scope gap is large — the FDE covers broader territory than the KSA, so the NLI model sees topical relatedness without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.5293) meets or exceeds the STS cross-encoder (0.4237). This is uncommon and suggests the semantic similarity is well-represented in the embedding space — the descriptions occupy similar vector-space regions even without joint processing.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (7.69% of tokens shared). Some shared vocabulary exists (likely common functional words or broadly-used domain terms) but the bulk of each description uses distinct terminology.
